sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application 385 of 2018 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.19 2. For hearing of main case 23.10.2025 Mr. Irfan Mir Halepota, advocate for the applicant On 29.08.2019 and 27.09.2019 time was sought to reformulate questions of law; same was not done. On 25.09.2020 and 07.05.2024 adjournment was sought. The order dated 07.05.2024 reads as follows: “07.05.2024 Mr. Irfan Mir Halepota, advocate for the applicant Counsel once again requests for adjournment to prepare his brief. Record reflects that time and again adjournment has been sought therefore, reluctantly, as a last and final chance, matter is adjourned with a note of caution that if no assistance is provided on the next date, no further indulgence would be granted. To come up on 28.05.2024” Today once again time is being sought. In view of judgment of the Supreme Court in the case of C.I.R. vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, this reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad
